description: Positioning beauty products as enhancers of natural features rather than replacements or transformations, emphasizing refinement and elevation of existing beauty.
structural_recipe: Lead with the phrase 'Your [feature], but better' or equivalent positioning. Show before/subtle-after visuals emphasizing enhancement of natural features. Use product name and functional benefits. Include bilingual captions (Arabic + English) with parallel messaging. Emphasize lifting, defining, and lasting qualities rather than dramatic change. Use fire emojis or enthusiasm markers to convey confidence without overselling.
why_it_works: Saudi audiences value authenticity and natural beauty enhanced rather than altered. This pattern respects cultural preferences for modest self-improvement while building confidence. It positions products as tools for self-expression within acceptable beauty norms rather than devices for fundamental transformation, reducing cognitive dissonance with conservative beauty values.
transplantation_caveats: Avoid language suggesting dramatic transformation or 'becoming someone else'
Do not imply dissatisfaction with natural features before product use
Maintain modest tone even while celebrating enhancement
Ensure bilingual execution maintains cultural message consistency
cultural_constraints: Must align with modest beauty standards
Enhancement framing preferred over transformation messaging
